title: sphenomeris flabellifolia
intro: Sphenomeris flabellifolia, also known as the fan-leaved spleenwort, is a type of fern native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Sphenomeris flabellifolia include its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce pain. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations may include dried leaves, roots, and stems, which can be consumed as tea or used in the form of a poultice.
